title: agrostis alba var. mutica
intro: Agrostis alba var. mutica, also known as redtop grass or purpletop grass, is a type of grass native to North America, characterized by its purple or reddish tint on the upper leaf sheath and panicle. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Agrostis alba var. mutica include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
